text.title: Docker Image Optimization (size + cache + supply chain) Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Final image is 1.2GB; carries gcc + python-dev + node_modules; only 80MB are actually used at runtime.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote a single-stage Dockerfile and never split build from runtime.
antipattern.fix: Split into `FROM python:3.12-slim AS builder` + `FROM gcr.io/distroless/python3-debian12 AS runtime`; `COPY --from=builder` only the wheel/binary; drop SDK.

antipattern.symptom: Build is 'reproducible' on paper but `python:latest` shifted from 3.11 to 3.12 between commits; runtime exception on tuple-syntax change.
antipattern.fix: Pin by digest: `FROM python:3.12-slim@sha256:abc...`; Renovate / Dependabot can bump the digest in a controlled PR.

antipattern.symptom: Container runs as root; CVE in app library combined with mount escape yields host compromise.
antipattern.root-cause: Author never added `USER` directive; default UID 0 stuck.
antipattern.fix: Add `RUN useradd -u 10001 -m app && USER 10001`; chown the working dir before USER switch; verify with `docker run --rm IMG id`.
